text: 32. Genesis chapter number 32. Genesis 32. I'm going to be looking this evening at the latter part of the passage of Scripture of this chapter, verses 24 through 32. And of course that caused Jacob to have a measure of concern because his last encounter with Esau was of course after he had stolen the blessing and the deception and all that took place and Esau said I'm going to kill him after I'm done mourning the death of my father. And of course mom told him to go away for a short time, it turned into over 20 years and we find that Jacob then hears this information and after hearing about Esau coming and having the men with him we find as we dealt with last week that he seeks the face of the Lord and then he prepares a present for Esau and strategically set up the camp. We're told in verse number 24 of our text of Scripture that the Bible says this and he took them, sorry in verse 24, and Jacob was left alone. Just pause there for a moment, we'll read the rest of the verse and down through the end of the chapter in just a moment but I want you to think about all that's just taken place as Jacob is seeking to obey the instruction that the Lord has given to return to the land of his fathers and he comes to this place, he sends these messengers, he hears that Esau's coming and the band of men that are with him and he's concerned, he's troubled, he has this thought that Esau's going to do him harm, perhaps even killing his wives and children and we find that Jacob is now in a position where the Bible says that he's left alone. There's a little doubt in my mind that Jacob is full of concern. I believe that if I could look in in this passage and thinking about Jacob and what we know about him, I would say that even if this did not transpire the way that it does, that it would be some trouble for Jacob to sleep this evening. The Bible tells us that when he's left there alone, notice what the Bible says in verse 24, And there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day. That's a long night. The Bible says in verse 25, And when he saw that he prevailed not against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh. The hollow of Jacob's thigh was out of joint as he wrestled with him. And he said, Let me go, for the day breaketh. And he said, I will not let thee go, except thou bless me. And he said unto him, What is thy name? And he said, Jacob. And he said, Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel. For as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed. And Jacob asked him and said, Tell me, I pray thee, thy name. And he said, Wherefore is it that thou dost ask my name? And he blessed him there. And Jacob called the name of the place Penuel. As we look at this text of Scripture, I believe that God gives us three specific things that would serve as reminders in Jacob's life. By the way, we need reminders in our lives.
